title: Arabidella procumbens
taxonomy: Arabidella procumbens is classified as a flowering plant within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This species is a part of the genus Arabidella, which is a group of plants native to Australia. Arabidella procumbens is a low-growing, spreading plant that typically thrives in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions. The classification of Arabidella procumbens is further detailed at the family, genus, and species levels, providing insight into its evolutionary relationships and characteristics. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, Arabidella procumbens shares similarities with other plants in this family, such as cabbage and broccoli.
